task_completion.description: Open or create the specified Jupyter notebook and load the data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Jupyter Notebook or JupyterLab interface open with the file path '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/missing_middle.ipynb'. The notebook should contain code reading from '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5'.

task_completion.description: Categorize residential units and filter zones with at least 100 units.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code cells showing logic to group/categorize units into 'Single-Family' (== 1), 'Missing Middle' (2-19), and 'High-Density' (>= 20), along with a filter ensuring total residential units per zone >= 100.

task_completion.description: Calculate percentages and flag opportunity zones based on criteria.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code cells calculating the percentage of each housing category and creating an 'is_opportunity_zone' flag where pct_single_family > 50.0 and pct_missing_middle < 15.0.

task_completion.description: Export the zone-level results to the specified CSV file with correct columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code showing a pandas DataFrame being saved to '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/missing_middle_zones.csv' with columns: zone_id, total_units, pct_single_family, pct_missing_middle, pct_high_density, and is_opportunity_zone. Alternatively, a file explorer or terminal showing this file exists.

task_completion.description: Generate a stacked bar chart for the top 20 opportunity zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code filtering for opportunity zones, sorting by 'total_units' descending, taking the top 20 (e.g., `.head(20)`), and plotting a stacked bar chart of the housing breakdown.

task_completion.description: Save the generated chart to the specified PNG path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code showing the plot being saved to '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/opportunity_zones_chart.png', or the image file visible in the file explorer/image viewer. The chart itself may also be visible in the notebook output.

integrity.description: Verify the notebook cells were actually executed.
integrity.visual_evidence: Jupyter notebook cells should have execution numbers (e.g., 'In [1]:', 'In [2]:') and display standard pandas/matplotlib outputs, rather than just being unexecuted text.

integrity.description: Verify the agent processed the actual HDF5 dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code must show pandas/HDFStore functions reading 'sanfran_public.h5'. There should be no hardcoded arrays or manual creation of the 29 opportunity zones.

integrity.description: Verify the CSV and PNG files were created programmatically.
integrity.visual_evidence: The notebook should contain the `to_csv()` and `savefig()` (or equivalent) commands pointing to the exact required output paths, rather than the agent manually creating files in a text editor or downloading them.
